findings.message: Applicant can bind the fintech to the official rules.
Solution is live with active customers.
Women customers are served and visible in metrics.
Gender-disaggregated data is collected and used.
Privacy, consent, and proportional data-use controls exist.
Entrant and applicant are outside comprehensively sanctioned jurisdictions.
Live partner metrics can replace synthetic sample data.
Women are not treated as a marketing segment only; the solution has GDD-backed women-customer evidence.
The solution can show at least one concrete path from trust signal to harm reduction.
Complaint and issue resolution are visible as customer-protection mechanisms.
Trust instrumentation covers multiple customer-journey moments instead of a narrow disclosure screen.
